description_long: This is a vintage manual coffee grinder with a square wooden base and a metal dome-shaped top. The grinder features a prominent, curved metal crank handle with a rounded wooden knob for turning, attached to the top center. The front side has a small pull-out drawer with a round wooden knob, used to collect the ground coffee. The wood is dark and aged, showing signs of wear and patina, while the metal components are darkened, likely from years of use. A small brass or metal nameplate is affixed to the front, though its inscription is not legible. The overall design is utilitarian and robust, typical of early 20th-century hand-operated grinders.
description: A vintage manual coffee grinder with a wooden box base, metal dome top, and a curved crank handle. It features a small front drawer for collecting grounds and shows signs of age and use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pull-out drawer with a round knob, the metal dome top, and the curved crank handle extending to the right.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the plain wooden back and the handle's knob, with no visible drawer or nameplate.
description_view_2: Left side view displaying the side of the wooden box and the extended crank handle, with the dome top and handle mechanism visible.
description_view_3: Rear-right view showing the back and side of the grinder, the dome top, and the crank handle from a different angle.
attribute_handle_design: The handle is a single, curved metal rod with a rounded wooden knob at the end, positioned on top of the grinder for manual rotation. The knob is smooth and bulbous, designed for comfortable gripping.
